message: bd-lff4p.10.8 implement deterministic flow-control policy engine

@@ -1323,6 +1323,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm VT/ANSI support matrix + conformance fixtures","description":"Goal
- Define exactly what FrankenTerm will implement (and what it will intentionally NOT implement) for VT/ANSI output streams.

Deliverables
- Support matrix for: C0/C1 controls, CSI sequences, SGR, OSC (incl OSC 8), DEC private modes, cursor modes, scrolling, insert/delete, tabs.
- A conformance fixture corpus (byte streams + expected grid snapshots) with deterministic replay.
- Clear "compat" boundaries: which behaviors must match de-facto terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Parser work can be driven directly from this matrix and fixtures.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449662450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449643043Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive VT/ANSI support matrix at docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md covering: C0/C1 controls, CSI (cursor, erase, insert/delete, scroll, modes), SGR (attributes, 16/256/RGB colors), OSC (title, hyperlinks, clipboard), DEC private modes, Kitty keyboard protocol, character sets. Includes 15 sections, explicit priority levels (Must/Should/Won't), parser state machine spec, conformance fixture format with 6 initial fixtures, and CI integration plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).
","notes":"2026-02-09 RainyMill follow-up: added scrollback_memory and resize_storm benchmark groups to parser_patch_bench, emitted machine-readable JSON events for memory/resize metrics, expanded bench_budget parser mappings and budgets for parser_throughput_large/full_pipeline/patch_diff_apply/resize_storm/scrollback_memory, and validated with cargo check/clippy/fmt plus ./scripts/bench_budget.sh --quick --json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RainyMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:06:02.018299494Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T01:57:59.666788056Z","close_reason":"Bench landed in 4cabfd1d; bd-lff4p.1.6 is closed; verified with cargo check -p frankenterm-core --benches","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:20.414665310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.11","title":"Fuzz: frankenterm-core parser and grid mutation entrypoints","description":"Goal
- Add fuzz targets to harden the parser and mutation logic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzz harnesses run locally and in CI (time-bounded) with crash artifacts captured.
- Any found crashes become regression fixtures in the conformance corpus.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","title":"Implement: new crate frankenterm-core (API skeleton + workspace wiring)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/frankenterm-core` with the minimal public API surface and internal module layout.

Notes
- Keep dependencies minimal.
- `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`.

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p frankenterm-core` passes (native).
- Basic types compile: Grid, Cell, Parser, Patch.
","notes":"Implemented crate skeleton in crates/frankenterm-core (Cell/SgrAttrs, Cursor+SavedCursor, Grid ops, Modes flags, Parser skeleton emitting Actions, Patch + Scrollback skeleton). Wired into workspace Cargo.toml. Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core, cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512397161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512374489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262569874Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:58:22.262549566Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:39.421707588Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","title":"Implement: scrollback + resize/reflow policy","description":"Goal
- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224829755Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:03:09.224807123Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:45.023051622Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.5","title":"E2E: scripted remote terminal sessions + golden transcripts","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end scripts that connect the browser FrankenTerm to the PTY bridge and run deterministic scripted sessions.

Requirements
- Scripts must emit detailed JSONL (run_id, git_sha, sizes, wire counters, checksums).
- Golden transcripts are checksum-gated; failures produce actionable diffs.

Acceptance Criteria
- At least 5 high-signal scripts: resize storm, paste, unicode, OSC8 links, and long scrollback.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","title":"Perf: keystroke-to-photon latency budget + measurement harness","description":"Goal
- Define and enforce latency budgets for interactive remote sessions.

Metrics
- Keystroke -> rendered frame (p50/p95/p99).
- Round-trip websocket latency and jitter.
- Output throughput under load.

Acceptance Criteria
- Harness outputs machine-readable JSON and is gated against regressions.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:08.237828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:08.237828576Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:08.237828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:08.237828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:08.237828576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","title":"Spec: TERM/capability profile + terminfo for FrankenTerm remote sessions","description":"Goal
- Ensure remote PTY apps behave correctly by defining FrankenTerm's terminal identity and capabilities.

Scope
- Decide what TERM will be set to in the PTY (e.g. `frankenterm`, `xterm-256color`, or a compatibility alias).
- If `TERM=frankenterm`, define and ship a terminfo entry (and a versioning story).
- Map terminfo capabilities to the VT/ANSI support matrix and reply engine behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- A pinned capability profile exists (colors, mouse, paste, focus, kitty, hyperlinks) with explicit "must" vs "best-effort" behaviors.
- E2E remote scripts validate that common CLI apps render correctly under the chosen TERM profile.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseStream","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:02:40.745909290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:02:40.745888390Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","title":"Alien Artifact: queueing-theoretic backpressure + fairness policy (remote protocol)","description":"Goal
- Design backpressure/flow-control for the websocket protocol so we never OOM and we maintain low interactive latency under load.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Queueing model for input/output queues (arrival/service rates, tail latency).
- Explicit objective: minimize expected loss with an asymmetric loss matrix:
  - huge penalty for OOM/unbounded memory
  - large penalty for keystroke latency spikes
  - smaller penalty for throughput loss
- Use an evidence ledger: record measured rates, chosen thresholds, and why.

Deliverables
- A spec for bounded queues + drop/merge policy (e.g. coalesce resize/mouse-move events, chunk outputs).
- A fairness policy so long outputs don't starve input (interactive priority).

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress harness demonstrates bounded memory and stable p95 keystroke latency under worst-case output floods.
- Policy is explainable: given metrics, we can show why a drop/coalesce happened.
","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:18:43.986007231Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- Design backpressure/flow-control for the websocket protocol so we never OOM and we maintain low interactive latency under load.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Queueing model for input/output queues (arrival/service rates, tail latency).
- Explicit objective: minimize expected loss with an asymmetric loss matrix:
  - huge penalty for OOM/unbounded memory
  - large penalty for keystroke latency spikes
  - smaller penalty for throughput loss
- Use an evidence ledger: record measured rates, chosen thresholds, and why.

Deliverables
- A spec for bounded queues + drop/merge policy (e.g. coalesce resize/mouse-move events, chunk outputs).
- A fairness policy so long outputs don't starve input (interactive priority).

Acceptance Criteria

- Policy is explainable: given metrics, we can show why a drop/coalesce happened.
","notes":"2026-02-09 JadePrairie: implemented deterministic flow-control/backpressure policy engine in crates/frankenterm-core/src/flow_control.rs with asymmetric loss scoring, fixed tie-break ordering, fairness monitor (Jain index), queue cap/drop helpers, output batch budgeting, and deterministic stress simulation tests for bounded queues + key-latency stability; linked spec section 4 to implementation target.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"JadePrairie","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:25:28.056052339Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:25:28.056025098Z","close_reason":"Completed: implemented deterministic flow-control/backpressure policy engine with stress invariants, fairness budgeting, and protocol alignment.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2","title":"FrankenTerm Web: WebGPU renderer + web input plumbing","description":"Scope
- Build `frankenterm-web`: a WebGPU-first terminal renderer + input stack compiled to WASM (no xterm.js).

User-Facing Requirements
- Crisp text + stable geometry under resize/zoom/DPR changes.
- Smooth scrollback (virtualized rendering; no O(n) over history per frame).
- Selection + copy/paste, OSC 8 hyperlinks (hover/click), and in-terminal search.
- Accessibility strategy despite canvas/WebGPU rendering (DOM mirror mode, focus, high-contrast, reduced motion).

Deliverables
- `frankenterm-web` crate exporting a minimal JS/WASM API: init, resize, input, render/present.
- WebGPU renderer (glyph atlas + instancing) with deterministic patch application.
- Input capture: keyboard (modifiers/repeat), mouse + wheel, touch; clipboard; IME/composition.
- Bench + E2E harnesses emitting detailed JSONL for correctness + perf gates.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:03.657124887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","title":"ADR: FrankenTerm WebGPU renderer architecture (glyph atlas, batching, present)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the renderer design for frankenterm-web.

Must Cover
- Rendering primitive set: background rects, text glyph quads, underline/strikethrough, cursor, selection.
- Glyph pipeline: bitmap vs SDF/MSDF; atlas packing; eviction; metrics.
- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.
- Color management: sRGB, blending rules for selections/cursor.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","title":"Bench: web renderer frame-time harness + regression thresholds","description":"Goal
- Build a repeatable web performance harness for FrankenTerm that supports the Extreme Optimization loop.

Metrics (minimum)
- Frame time histogram (p50/p95/p99) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Time-to-first-frame and time-to-interactive.
- Patch statistics: bytes per frame, dirty-span counts, draw-call counts.
- Memory usage (JS + WASM) steady-state and after stress (scrollback/resize).
- GPU timings when available (best-effort).

Outputs
- Machine-readable JSON for CI gating and local profiling.
- Optional JSONL stream for per-frame/per-event diagnostics (run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR).

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline runs are stable enough to detect regressions (document expected variance + thresholds).
- Harness can be driven by deterministic traces (record/replay) so profiles are comparable.

 To prevent flooding, the server and client MUST coalesce bursty events:
